exampleSentences: Ngày mai anh phải đi làm công chuyện ở văn phòng từ sáng đến chiều.
Chị đang bận công chuyện nên không thể đi chơi với bạn được.
Em thường giúp mẹ làm công chuyện nhà vào cuối tuần.
notes:
công chuyện = work, business to take care of, errands. It often means personal or practical matters you need to handle, not just a job.

It’s an uncountable noun — you don’t use classifiers with it. Common patterns: có công chuyện (have something to deal with), đi làm công chuyện (go handle work/business).

Very close to công việc, but công việc sounds more neutral and formal; công chuyện feels more casual and everyday.

Dialect: much more common in the South; in the North, people usually say công việc instead.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Tomorrow I have to go to the office for work from morning to evening.
She is busy with work so she cannot hang out with her friend.
I often help my mom with work at home on the weekends.
notes_md:
**công chuyện** = work, business to take care of, errands. It often means personal or practical matters you need to handle, not just a job.

It’s an uncountable noun — you don’t use classifiers with it. Common patterns: *có công chuyện* (have something to deal with), *đi làm công chuyện* (go handle work/business).

Very close to **công việc**, but *công việc* sounds more neutral and formal; *công chuyện* feels more casual and everyday.

Dialect: much more common in the South; in the North, people usually say **công việc** instead.
